results.title: Beyond Babel. Program 2. Culture
results.creator_credit: Mike Fiddler, Ron Blythe, Sally Westbury and Infonation
results.brief_description: With the advent of the 21st century came the unstoppable global spread of English as the universal language. “Beyond Babel” explores the reasons behind this phenomenal growth through four separate programs: Trade, Culture, Politics, Future. In this second program, the issue of the English language and its influence upon a country’s culture is discussed. Britain’s past colonial exploits has left an indelible imprint on many of her colonies with the spread of the English language through religion and literature. With globalised media bombardment 24 hours a day in every conceivable form, religious broadcasts are seen and heard world-wide in English. American tele-evangelist Pat Robertson uses satellite television to spread the gospel to over 165 countries. African Americans have developed their own English dialect through the Baptist Church and black music (rap and hip hop) and in South Africa, black and white actors collaborate in theatre performance using Shakespearean English in an African way to highlight issues of apartheid. Black South Africans use English as a weapon to fight the “white master”. In India, as a result of British colony influence, the universities teach English literature (Shakespeare, Byron etc) and English is considered as an Indian language, particularly from the youth point of view - it is “uncool” to speak Hindi. Even young Bollywood filmmakers are moving away from traditional Hindi and using English in their films. Shows Tokyo as an example of the western influence that has invaded Asia, highlighting fashion, music and the global club culture where English is “the” language. Narrated by Hugh Quarshie.

results.title: Beyond Babel. Program 1. Trade
results.creator_credit: Mike Fiddler, Ron Blythe, Sally Westbury and Infonation
results.brief_description: With the advent of the 21st century came the unstoppable global spread of English as the universal language. “Beyond Babel” explores the reasons behind this phenomenal growth through four separate programs: Trade, Culture, Politics, Future. In this first program, we see the effects of globalisation and international trade on such countries as China, India, Africa and South Africa and how the English language has become a link to the outside world for the poorer nations. In recognition of the power of the English language, many non-English speaking countries are teaching English as a second language to their school children in order to help in advancing their careers, particularly in Africa and India. Shows examples of the power of global brands (American), i.e. Coca-Cola, Levis, McDonalds and how English unifies the corporate business on an international level, particularly in the recruitment of staff - all staff, whether in China, India, Africa, are required to speak some level of English. The question is asked, “has English banished nationhood in favour of global branding?”. The dominance of English in the financial markets where words or “mainstream jargon” cross all international boundaries and globalisation equals connectivity, the subject of dependence by world businesses on the lingua franca of international business English is discussed. Narrated by Hugh Quarshie.

results.title: Best of British: Triumph
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Best of British”.
Triumph is one of the greatest names in British motorcycle history. This documentary follows the story of the Triumph motorcycle from its beginnings in Coventry just after the turn of the century, to its resurrection in the late eighties. The turbulent history of the Triumph company is revealed through interviews with key figures associated with the Triumph name and significant Triumph models of the past and present are discussed in detail.

results.title: Best of British: the official red arrows 1995/96 World Tour
results.creator_credit: Tony Wall, Jonathon McNicholas and R.E. W
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Best of British”.
Witness the Royal Air Force Aerobatic Team, the Red Arrows, in their distinctive red British Aerospace Hawk aircraft, achieve the epitome of flying and technical proficiency. This program follows the team to locations in the Middle East, South Africa, Zimbabwe and Australia as they perform precisely choreographed maneuvers in their spectacular aerial displays.

results.title: Best of British: MG
results.creator_credit: Bruce Cox and Syncro Sports Television
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Best of British”.
Twenty years after the MG factory was closed down, its parent company successfully revived the make. This documentary takes a detailed look at every signigicant MG ever made, from the earliest Midget in the twenties through the T-types, the MG-A and the company’s biggest seller so far, the MG-B. It also includes interviews with enthusiasts and designers, a visit to MG headquarters and rare archival footage of races and rallies.

results.title: Beyond 2000 Climate in Crisis
results.brief_description: The Greenhouse Effect is the greatest environmental issue to hit the world this century. Its ultimate consequences could be second only to a nuclear war. Created by the Beyond 2000 group, “Climate in Crisis” comprises six segments. In this special report the phenomenon, the Greenhouse Effect, is examined from a global perspective featuring both British and Australian correspondents. Rated: G

results.brief_description: This 8 part series looks at some of the classic cars that have been produced in Great Britain, and have brought years of driving pleasure to fine automobile enthusiasts.

results.title: Best of British: Land Rover
results.creator_credit: Bruce Cox and Synchro Sports Television
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Best of British”.
This documentary traces the history of the Land Rover through prototypes and production models. The program travels to the locations where Land Rover made its name as a rugged off-road vehicle such as the Andes of South America and the jungles of Belize, and visits the largest Land Rover rally in the world.

results.title: Best of British: Jaguar
results.creator_credit: Bruce Cox and Synchro Sports Television
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Best of British”.
An early Jaguar advertising slogan summed up the timeless appeal of this great British marque as ‘grace, space and pace’. This documentary explores these legendary qualities by taking a close look at all the significant Jaguar models, from the pre-war coach-built ‘special saloons’ through the SS100 and XK120, the E-type and XJ models, up to Britain’s first ‘supercar’- the XJ220. Interviews with Jaguar drivers such as Stirling Moss and Jack Fairman, and archival footage contribute to a fascinating review of Jaguar’s unique place in British motoring history.
